frameworkName: Accounting and Auditing Organisation for Islamic Financial Institutions — IT Governance and Sharia Compliance Standards
applicability: Islamic banks, Islamic windows of conventional banks, takaful operators, Islamic investment companies, and financial institutions offering Sharia-compliant products in GCC, Pakistan, Malaysia, and global Islamic finance markets

controls.title: Sharia Supervisory Board Oversight of Technology
controls.description: Any technology system used by an Islamic financial institution to execute or record financial transactions must be reviewed and approved by the Sharia Supervisory Board to ensure conformity with Sharia principles.
requiredEvidence: Sharia Supervisory Board approval requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) citing AAOIFI Governance Standards
SSB review and approval process referenced in project scope documentation
Sharia compliance sign-off milestone referenced in SRS constraint requirements

controls.title: Prohibition of Riba in System Logic
controls.description: Software systems must not embed or calculate interest-based returns (riba) in any financial transaction logic. Profit-sharing, markup, and lease-based transaction models must be implemented per AAOIFI Sharia Standards.
requiredEvidence: Transaction logic description confirming no interest calculation in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Profit-sharing and markup calculation models described in SDS Component Specifications section
Sharia-compliant transaction type definitions in SRS functional requirements with acceptance criteria

controls.title: Zakat Calculation and Distribution Module
controls.description: Islamic financial institutions with asset management functions must provide a Zakat calculation engine compliant with AAOIFI FAS standards, enabling accurate calculation and distribution of Zakat obligations.
requiredEvidence: Zakat calculation requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) where asset management is in scope
Zakat engine component described in SDS Component Specifications section where applicable
AAOIFI FAS Zakat standard reference cited in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Sharia Audit Trail for Financial Transactions
controls.description: All financial transactions in Islamic banking systems must maintain a complete and immutable audit trail enabling Sharia auditors to verify compliance of each transaction with approved Sharia contract types.
requiredEvidence: Sharia audit trail requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*) and constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Audit logging component with Sharia contract type tagging described in SDS Security Architecture — Audit Logging subsection
Sharia auditor access and reporting functionality described in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Accounting Standards Compliance (AAOIFI FAS)
controls.description: Financial reporting and accounting modules must comply with AAOIFI Financial Accounting Standards (FAS), including correct recognition and measurement of Islamic finance instruments such as murabaha, ijarah, musharakah, and sukuk.
requiredEvidence: AAOIFI FAS compliance requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Financial reporting module and accounting treatment described in SDS Component Specifications section
AAOIFI accounting standard references cited in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)

controls.title: Profit and Loss Sharing Calculation Accuracy
controls.description: Systems implementing mudarabah, musharakah, or diminishing musharakah contracts must calculate profit and loss sharing ratios with full auditability, traceable to the approved contract terms.
requiredEvidence: Profit and loss sharing calculation logic documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) with acceptance criteria
Calculation engine for mudarabah/musharakah described in SDS Component Specifications section
Audit trail for profit distribution decisions referenced in SDS Security Architecture — Audit Logging subsection

controls.title: Ethical Investment Screening (Halal Filter)
controls.description: Investment and portfolio management systems must implement an ethical screening engine that prevents investment in industries prohibited by Sharia, including alcohol, tobacco, conventional finance, and weapons.
requiredEvidence: Ethical investment screening requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) where investment management is in scope
Halal filter component described in SDS Component Specifications section with prohibited sector list
Screening update mechanism and data source referenced in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Islamic Finance Product Documentation
controls.description: Every Islamic financial product offered through the system must be backed by AAOIFI-compliant product documentation including approved contract templates, Sharia certificates, and fatwa references.
requiredEvidence: Product documentation requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Product document management component described in SDS Component Specifications section
Sharia certificate storage and versioning referenced in SDS Database Schema section

controls.title: Displaced Commercial Risk and Rate of Return Risk
controls.description: Systems managing investment accounts must implement controls to identify and report displaced commercial risk and rate of return risk per AAOIFI Governance Standard No. 1.
keywords: rate of return risk Islamic bank
requiredEvidence: Displaced commercial risk and rate of return risk controls documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*) where investment accounts are in scope
Risk calculation and reporting component described in SDS Component Specifications section
AAOIFI Governance Standard No. 1 cited in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Islamic Financial Reporting and Disclosure
controls.description: Financial reporting systems must support AAOIFI-compliant financial statements including statements of financial position, income, changes in equity, cash flows, and restricted and unrestricted investment accounts.
requiredEvidence: AAOIFI financial reporting requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Financial reporting module and statement generation described in SDS Component Specifications section
AAOIFI standard references cited in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
